```python
# test_count_vowels.py
import pytest

from your_module import count_vowels  # adjust import to match your project


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "s, expected",
    [
        ("hello", 2),          # e, o
        ("WORLD", 1),          # o
        ("aeiou", 5),          # all vowels
        ("AEIOU", 5),          # uppercase handled
        ("", 0),               # empty string
        ("bcdfg", 0),          # no vowels
        ("aEiOu", 5),          # mixed case
        ("   a   e   ", 2),    # whitespace preserved; vowels still counted
        ("naïve", 3),          # note: ï is NOT counted by this implementation
        ("café", 2),          # é is NOT counted by this implementation; only a counted
    ],
)
def test_count_vowels_happy_and_edge_cases(s, expected):
    assert count_vowels(s) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize("s", [None, 123, 45.6, ["a", "e"], {"a": "e"}])
def test_count_vowels_error_cases_non_string_inputs(s):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        count_vowels(s)


def test_count_vowels_raises_on_missing_lower_attribute():
    class NoLower:
        pass

    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        count_vowels(NoLower())
```
